---
author: Connor Aird
institute: University College London
date: 2026-01-31
format: revealjs
subtitle: "ARC Forum"
title: Pytest Benchmark, was it worth it?
logo: /assets/ucl_logo_light.png
recording:
---

{{< include _slides/re-intro.qmd >}}

{{< include _slides/project-context.qmd >}}

{{< include _slides/what-is-pytest-benchmark.qmd >}}

# How we used pytest-benchmark

- Initialy use separate repo. Eventually merged into single repo.
- Reuse fixtures from core tests.
- Define Regression tests which compare base ref to HEAD ref for PRs.
  - Benchmarks used are those from the HEAD ref.
  - Run in GitHub actions
  - Both runs occur right after one another so in theory experince the same load
    on the machine.

# The issues

- If the GLASS api chnged (i.e. new module) the regression tests would fail.
- Flaky regression tests
  - Split into stable and unstable with different metrics for each.
  - Maximise problem size.
  - Filter to only run for NumPy.

# Conclusion

- Not clear if it was worth it.
- Has definitely highlighted regressions.
- We hope to use it in the future
  - Demonstrate GPU improvements.
  - Benchmark machines.
